note: Sibling 1 of 3, built to test whether the float-money blind spot is a one-off or a family: 'the code uses a nearly-right operation, and the judge accepts nearly as exactly'. Here the nearly-right operation is floor division, which silently drops the remainder. Aimed deliberately at a known weakness rather than hunted at random. Every claim below was verified by running it.

labels.text: the amounts always add up to exactly the total
labels.why: verified: split_bill(100, 3) returns [33, 33, 33], which sums to 99. A penny vanishes on every bill that does not divide evenly. Note it passes the obvious test: split_bill(90, 3) sums to exactly 90, so a test written with round numbers is green.

labels.text: no two people are asked for amounts differing by more than one penny
labels.why: verified: every amount is identical, so the largest difference is 0. This criterion genuinely is met, and it is what makes the code look reasonable.
